steps.title: Identify the antecedent
steps.content: The dependent clause establishes “a given industry” (singular) as its subject. The main clause needs a pronoun that correctly refers to this singular noun.

steps.title: Apply standard pronoun reference
steps.content: “It” is the standard form for referring back to a previously mentioned singular noun, and “it often consists” correctly establishes pronoun-antecedent agreement with “industry.” The other options either use plural pronouns (they, these) that don't match the singular antecedent, or use “this,” a less standard choice. That matches choice A — A is correct.
